task_completion.description: The flower image is opened in an image editor.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An image editor application (like GIMP) is open and displaying a photograph of a flower.

task_completion.description: The elliptical selection tool is selected.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Ellipse Select tool is highlighted or selected in the image editor's toolbox.

task_completion.description: An elliptical selection is created around the flower.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An elliptical selection boundary (often shown as 'marching ants' or a dashed line) is visible on the image, encompassing the flower.

task_completion.description: The stroke settings are configured for an 8-pixel red line.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Stroke Selection' dialog or similar settings panel is visible, showing the line width set to 8 pixels and the color set to red.

task_completion.description: An 8-pixel red elliptical stroke outline is applied to the flower.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A solid red elliptical outline is clearly visible on the image, matching the shape of the previous selection.

integrity.description: The agent used the image editor's GUI to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the image editor's menus, toolbars, and dialogs, rather than terminal commands executing CLI image processing tools.

integrity.description: The agent used the stroke selection feature rather than manually painting.
integrity.visual_evidence: The stroke appears perfectly elliptical and uniform, and screenshots show the use of a 'Stroke Selection' dialog rather than manual brush strokes.

integrity.description: The stroke is applied to the provided flower image.
integrity.visual_evidence: The red stroke is applied directly on top of the original flower photograph, not on a blank canvas or a different image.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires applying a stroke to a real flower photograph (800x533 JPEG). The selection must be elliptical. The stroke must be pure red (#FF0000) and have a width of 8 pixels. The final output is expected to be saved as 'flower_stroke.jpg', though the primary visual requirement is the presence of the 8-pixel red elliptical outline on the flower.
